label: UK National Debt Increase (1913–1920)
source: UK National Debt Office / Morgan & Morgan, The Costs of War (1993)

label: German Mark vs. Dollar (1914–1923)
change: Hyperinflation driven by war reparations and war debts
source: Bundesbank Historical Statistics / Niall Ferguson, Paper and Iron (1995)

label: Artillery Shells Fired (All Belligerents)
change: Production rose from 2,000/month to 200,000/month in UK alone
source: Hew Strachan, The First World War (2003) / Official Histories

label: Women Entering UK Workforce (1914–1918)
change: Women's employment rose 46% in munitions alone
source: UK Board of Trade, Labour Statistics / Susan Grayzel, Women's Identities at War (1999)

label: WWI Reparations Imposed on Germany
change: ~$400 billion in 2021 USD; final payment made October 2010
source: Reparations Commission 1921 / Germany made final payment October 3, 2010

label: Allied Merchant Ships Lost to U-boats
change: Peak: 860,000 tons lost in April 1917 alone
source: UK Admiralty Records / Robert Massie, Castles of Steel (2003)

label: UK Munitions Workers (Peak)
change: Lloyd George's Ministry of Munitions transformed industrial output
source: Ministry of Munitions, History of the Great War (1922)

label: US Loans to Allied Powers (1914–1917)
change: US neutrality effectively underwritten Allied war finance
source: Federal Reserve Bank of New York / Kathleen Burk, Britain, America and the Sinews of War (1985)

label: Europe's Share of World Industrial Output
change: US rose from 32% to 42% of world industrial output
source: Angus Maddison, The World Economy: Historical Statistics (2003)

label: German Civilian Calorie Intake (1916–1918)
source: Belinda Davis, Home Fires Burning: Food, Politics and Everyday Life in WWI Berlin (2000)
